pages.text:
DRAFT SPECIFICATION
SILICONE RUBBER INSULATOR COATINGS
GENERAL
Insulator coating materials must be of room temperature vulcanising (RTV) silicone rubber
formulation, specifically developed for use on outdoor high voltage porcelain, glass, resin
and rubber insulation. The coating must be easily applied using standard spray-painting
equipment and yield a naturally smooth finish.
CHARACTERISTICS
In addition to being highly hydrophobic, other necessary design characteristics are:
• fast hydrophobicity transfer to contaminating layers
• fast recovery of hydrophobicity after partial discharge activity
• excellent arc resistance
• resistance to damage by corona discharge
• resistance to deterioration from ultraviolet (UV) radiation
• resistance to degradation by atmospheric and chemical contaminants such
as marine salts and industrial pollutants
• good adhesion to glass, ceramic and resin surfaces
PROPERTIES
The coating should possess the properties specified in the table below.
PROPERTY TEST METHOD UNIT VALUE
Specific gravity @ 23°C 0,9 to 1.25
Dielectric strength @ 23°C BS 2918 kV/mm >50
Surface resistivity IEC 60003 ohm >1.25 x 1013
Volume resistivity IEC 60093 ohm metre >2.25 x 1013
Hydrophobicity IEC 62073 contact angle >90°
Arc resistance ASTM D 495-99 seconds >185
Tracking and Erosion ASTM D 2303-97 minutes @ 2.5kV >470
Hydrophobicity Recovery IEC 62073 Wettability class: HC1-HC2 within
100 hrs corona HC1 - HC6 48 hrs
Adhesion ISO 16276-2 Class 1
Adhesion IEC 62217 No loss of
100 hrs boiling adhesion
Skin-over time 23° C minutes <25

pages.text:
COMPOSITION
In view of the more controlled curing rate and the extended shelf life, two-part silicone
rubber compositions are preferred. The shelf life, when stored at temperatures between
5°C and 30°C must be a minimum of 12 months.
After curing, the silicone rubber content must be more than 50% by weight.
The filler used to ensure partial arc resistance must be alumina trihydrate.
The coating must be supplied with a suitable primer to ensure excellent adhesion to
glass, ceramic, resin and rubber substrates.
PERFORMANCE
The material must have displayed satisfactory and maintenance-free service performance
over a period at least 10 years at sites of at least “Very Heavy” IEC pollution severity
classification.
In addition to the above, the coating must have passed a performance evaluation of a
minimum of one year duration at a naturally polluted insulator test station as per the Eskom
Procedure DPC 34-224 or similar.
PACKING
The material must be available in 1 litre and 5 litre tins accompanied by pre-measured
volumes of catalyst and primer. The tins must be marked with a batch number and the
manufacturing date.
